name: Colorado Artificial Intelligence Act
description: Colorado state law (SB 24-205, signed 2024-05-17, effective 2026-02-01) regulating use of high-risk AI systems to prevent algorithmic discrimination. Requires developers and deployers of high-risk AI systems to use reasonable care to avoid algorithmic discrimination. Deployers must conduct impact assessments, provide notice to consumers, and implement risk management policies. Developers must provide documentation, known limitations, and usage guidelines. Covers AI systems making consequential decisions in education, employment, financial services, government services, healthcare, housing, insurance, and legal services. Attorney General enforcement.
relationToCloudSecurity: AI systems deployed via cloud and used to make consequential decisions about Colorado residents fall under the Act. Cloud-based AI vendors selling to Colorado deployers must support deployer compliance.
relationToAISecurity: First US state law explicitly establishing AI governance obligations beyond pre-existing privacy/civil rights frameworks. Major reference for emerging US state AI regulation.

license.notes: US state statutes are public-domain government works and may be freely redistributed.

current_extraction.notes: Marker-extracted PDF + custom CRS-section parser (extract_co_ai_act.py). Output: ai-act-sections.{csv,json} with 7 CRS sections (6-1-1701 Definitions through 6-1-1707 Rules).

extraction_history.reason: Structured extraction of the 7 CRS sections that the bill enacts

notes: First-of-kind US state AI law. Acquire signed bill PDF from leg.colorado.gov for structured extraction.
